name: ssl
description: SSL/TLS socket wrapping for secure network communication. Includes SSLContext for certificate management. Requires mbedtls firmware support.
differences_from_cpython: Very minimal compared to CPython's ssl module.
Supports TLS client, TLS server, DTLS client, DTLS server.

functions.name: ssl.wrap_socket
functions.signature: wrap_socket(sock, *, server_side=False, keyfile=None, certfile=None, server_hostname=None, cert_reqs=ssl.CERT_NONE, ca_certs=None)
functions.description: Wrap an existing TCP socket with TLS/SSL encryption.

functions.name: ssl.MBEDTLS_VERSION
functions.description: String with mbedTLS library version (e.g., 'Mbed TLS 3.6.4'). Read-only attribute.

methods.name: SSLContext.load_cert_chain
methods.description: Load certificate + private key for server mode.

methods.name: SSLContext.load_verify_locations
methods.description: Load CA certificate for client verification.

example:
import socket
import ssl

sock = socket.socket()
addr = socket.getaddrinfo('httpbin.org', 443)[0][-1]
sock.connect(addr)
ssl_sock = ssl.wrap_socket(sock, server_hostname='httpbin.org')
ssl_sock.write(b'GET /get HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: httpbin.org\r\n\r\n')
print(ssl_sock.read(200))
ssl_sock.close()
esp32_notes: TLS support requires MICROPY_SSL_MBEDTLS=y in firmware (most builds have it).
For HTTPS, consider using the 'requests' module (mip install requests).
